title: wtf-worker: Define Worker struct with storage, master, registry
description: Define the Worker struct with Arc<sled::Db>, ActorRef<OrchestratorMsg>, and ActivityRegistry. Implements Send + Sync for thread-safety.

postconditions: Worker can be cloned and shared across tasks

questions: How does sled::Db provide Send + Sync?

phase_1: Define Worker struct with proper Arc wrappers

title: wtf-worker: Implement dequeue_workflow from run_queue
description: Poll the run_queue in storage for next invocation_id. Uses tokio::sync::mpsc channel or sled市民trees. Returns Option<InvocationId>

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL check run_queue on each loop iteration

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return the next invocation_id

happy: dequeue returns invocation_id when queue has entries
error: dequeue returns None when queue empty
dequeue fails if queue tree corrupted

phase_2: Add tokio::select! bias for better latency

title: wtf-worker: Implement check_expired_timers poll mechanism
description: Poll the timers tree for expired timers. Each timer has a deadline. Returns Option<Timer> for next expired timer.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL check expired timers on each loop iteration

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return the timer for delivery

preconditions: current time is available via tokio::time::Instant
postconditions: Returns Some(Timer) if any timer expired
Returns None if no timers expired

happy: check_expired_timers returns timer when deadline passed
error: check_expired_timers returns None when no timers expired

patterns: timer wheel vs sorted tree approach
questions: How to handle timer precision vs polling overhead?

phase_1: Implement check_expired_timers using min deadline lookup

title: wtf-worker: Implement check_pending_signals poll mechanism
description: Poll signals tree for pending signals destined for workflows. Signals include: cancellation, suspension, wakeup. Returns Option<Signal>.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL check pending signals on each loop iteration

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL return the signal for delivery

postconditions: Returns Some(Signal) with valid target invocation_id
Returns None if no pending signals
invariants: Signal.target points to valid workflow invocation

error: check_pending_signals returns None when no signals

title: wtf-worker: Implement process_workflow to continue execution
description: Signal workflow to continue after dequeue. Looks up workflow state, replays journal, executes pending commands. Updates workflow state after completion.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL continue workflow execution after dequeue

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL signal workflow to process next step

invariants: Journal entries are immutable and append-only

questions: How to handle non-deterministic command output?

title: wtf-worker: Implement handle_timer_fired delivery
description: Deliver timer fired event to the target workflow. Timer contains invocation_id and timer_type. Workflow processes timer and may schedule more work.

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver TimerFired event to target workflow

happy: handle_timer_fired delivers event to correct workflow

questions: How to handle timer for already-completed workflow?

phase_2: Remove timer from tree after delivery

title: wtf-worker: Implement deliver_signal to workflow
description: Deliver pending signal to target workflow. Signal types: Cancellation (workflow stops), Suspension (workflow pauses), Wakeup (workflow resumes).

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL stop workflow execution
THE SYSTEM SHALL pause workflow at current step
THE SYSTEM SHALL resume workflow from suspension point

invariants: Signal delivered exactly once to target

happy: deliver_signal delivers Cancellation and workflow stops

questions: What's the interaction between signals and running workflows?

phase_1: Implement deliver_signal for each signal type

title: wtf-worker: Implement main run loop with tokio::select
description: Main event loop using tokio::select! to multiplex between dequeue_workflow, check_expired_timers, and check_pending_signals. Enforces 3x parallelism limit from ADR-009.

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL run until explicitly stopped
THE SYSTEM SHALL check all three sources each iteration

happy: run loop processes workflow when available
error: run loop recovers from storage errors

questions: How to handle select! with multiple ready branches?

phase_0: Design shutdown mechanism (drop signal, cancellation token)
